articles.title: Snapchat Investigated in Europe Over Child Safety Policies
articles.summary: Regulators in Brussels accused the social media platform of maintaining a weak age-verification system, and steering younger users toward inappropriate experiences.

articles.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

articles.title: Who Is Alireza Tangsiri, the Latest Iranian Commander Targeted by Israel?
articles.summary: Israel said it killed the naval commander, Alireza Tangsiri, in an airstrike on Thursday morning. Iran has not commented.

articles.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial
articles.summary: Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.

articles.title: A Landmark Verdict on the Danger of Social Media, and Trump’s Call to Punish ‘Rogue Judges’
articles.summary: Plus, OnlyFans’ billionaire owner dies at 43.

articles.title: Israel and Iran ramp up attacks as Trump insists Tehran wants a deal
articles.summary: The war in the Middle East ramped up on Thursday as Israel launched a wave of strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure, and Iran fired rounds of missiles at central Israel.

articles.title: Trump's attacks on offshore wind could hurt infrastructure spending across the economy
articles.summary: President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.

articles.title: Juries Take the Lead in the Push for Child Online Safety
articles.summary: A pair of verdicts held social media companies accountable for harming young users, highlighting a growing backlash as Congress struggles to pass legislation.

articles.title: Some Judges See Risks in Fiery Opinions Warning of Threats to Democracy
articles.summary: Federal judges are weighing strategies for how to respond to the high stakes, anonymous threats and politicized atmosphere of the Trump era.

articles.title: It's Equal Pay Day. Women have lost ground for the second year in a row
articles.summary: The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.

articles.title: A $50 billion fund to help rural hospitals could actually lead to closures and cuts
articles.summary: States are rolling out plans for their share of a $50 billion fund established by Congress to improve rural health care. In some states, the money may provoke rural hospitals to cut services.

articles.title: Decades ago, a Maryland sailor burned his winter socks. Now it's a spring tradition
articles.summary: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by burning their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.

articles.title: Hoping to unseat Collins, Maine Democrats battle it out in an expensive U.S. Senate primary
articles.summary: As June's primary election nears, Democratic Gov. Janet Mills and combat veteran Graham Platner are effectively engaged in a proxy battle between factions in their own party.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Iran rejects U.S. peace proposal and lays out its own conditions, the Army's 82nd Airborne Division readies to deploy to Iran, jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial.

articles.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
articles.summary: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.

articles.title: Trump’s Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
articles.summary: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America’s war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease a

articles.title: Republicans in Congress Fret Over Trump Administration’s Handling of Iran War
articles.summary: G.O.P. lawmakers who have given the Trump administration wide latitude to wage war with no congressional input are growing frustrated as officials offer little detail about ground troops, cost or time

articles.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
articles.summary: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.

articles.title: How Kharg Island May Change the Trajectory of the Iran War
articles.summary: Kharg Island exports 90 percent of Iran’s crude oil. It has also become a potential U.S. target. Peter Eavis, our Business reporter, examines how the small island in the Persian Gulf has become a stra

articles.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.

articles.title: Greetings from Turkey's border with Iran, where Iranians let loose on the dance floor
articles.summary: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.

articles.title: For the first time in more than 1,400 years, Church of England gets a woman leader
articles.summary: A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
